revision.text:
'''Hakujōshi Army Aviation School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校)
== Overview == The '''Hakujōshi Army Aviation School''' was one of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in {{ill|1939|ja|昭和14年}} (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently {{ill|Tokorozawa|ja|所沢市}} Namiki), {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. Later, it moved to Heitai near {{ill|Baicheng City|ja|白城市}}, in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国}}).
In June {{ill|1944|ja|昭和19年}}, the school was relocated to {{ill|Haga District, Tochigi|ja|芳賀郡}}, currently part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}} ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in {{ill|1933|ja|昭和8年}} at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from {{ill|1935|ja|昭和10年}} at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}, with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14年「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14年(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In [[Category:January]][[Category:Search created pages]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]]1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the {{ill|Aviation Inspectorate General|ja|陸軍航空総監部}}<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * {{ill|Ikuhiko Hata|ja|秦郁彦}}, ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years).
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}

Both were disbanded following the end of the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}} ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争}}) in August of the same year. == History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in {{ill|1933|ja|昭和8年}} at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from {{ill|1935|ja|昭和10年}} at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}, with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14年「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14年(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In [[Category:January]][[Category:Search created pages]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-The Baicheng Army Flying School-qq]] [[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]]1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the {{ill|Aviation Inspectorate General|ja|陸軍航空総監部}}<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * {{ill|Ikuhiko Hata|ja|秦郁彦}}, ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years).

In [[Category:January]][[Category:Search created pages]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-The Baicheng Army Flying School-qq]] [[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]]1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the {{ill|Aviation Inspectorate General|ja|陸軍航空総監部}}<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.

'''Hakujōshi Army Aviation School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校)
== Overview == The '''Hakujōshi Army Aviation School''' was one of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in {{ill|1939|ja|昭和14年}} (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently {{ill|Tokorozawa|ja|所沢市}} Namiki), {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. Later, it moved to Heitai near {{ill|Baicheng City|ja|白城市}}, in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国}}).
In June {{ill|1944|ja|昭和19年}}, the school was relocated to {{ill|Haga District, Tochigi|ja|芳賀郡}}, currently part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}} ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in {{ill|1933|ja|昭和8年}} at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from {{ill|1935|ja|昭和10年}} at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}, with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14年「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14年(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In [[Category:January]][[Category:Search created pages]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-The Baicheng Army Flying School-qq]] [[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]]1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the {{ill|Aviation Inspectorate General|ja|陸軍航空総監部}}<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.

== References == * {{ill|Ikuhiko Hata|ja|秦郁彦}}, ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years).

[[Category:Imperial Japanese Army educational institutions]] [[Category:Manchukuo military facilities]] [[Category:Defunct aviation schools in Japan]] [[Category:Baicheng history]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-The Baicheng Army Flying School-qq]] [[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] [[ja:白城子陸軍飛行学校]]

'''The Baicheng Army Flying School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校) was one of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in {{ill|1939|ja|昭和14年}} (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently {{ill|Tokorozawa|ja|所沢市}} Namiki), {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. Later, it moved to Heitai near {{ill|Baicheng City|ja|白城市}}, in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国}}).
In June {{ill|1944|ja|昭和19年}}, the school was relocated to {{ill|Haga District, Tochigi|ja|芳賀郡}}, currently part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}} ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in {{ill|1933|ja|昭和8年}} at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from {{ill|1935|ja|昭和10年}} at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}, with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14年「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14年(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In [[Category:January]][[Category:Search created pages]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-The Baicheng Army Flying School-qq]] [[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]]1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the {{ill|Aviation Inspectorate General|ja|陸軍航空総監部}}<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * {{ill|Ikuhiko Hata|ja|秦郁彦}}, ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years).
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== Related Topics ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''The Baicheng Army Flying School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校) was one of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in {{ill|1939|ja|昭和14年}} (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently {{ill|Tokorozawa|ja|所沢市}} Namiki), {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. Later, it moved to Heitai near {{ill|Baicheng City|ja|白城市}}, in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国}}).
In June {{ill|1944|ja|昭和19年}}, the school was relocated to {{ill|Haga District, Tochigi|ja|芳賀郡}}, currently part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}} ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in {{ill|1933|ja|昭和8年}} at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from {{ill|1935|ja|昭和10年}} at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}, with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14年「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14年(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In [[Category:January]][[Category:Search created pages]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-The Baicheng Army Flying School-qq]] [[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]]1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the {{ill|Aviation Inspectorate General|ja|陸軍航空総監部}}<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * {{ill|Ikuhiko Hata|ja|秦郁彦}}, ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years).
== See Also == * {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}

[[Category:Imperial Japanese Army educational institutions]] [[Category:Manchukuo military facilities]] [[Category:Defunct aviation schools in Japan]] [[Category:Baicheng history]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--The Baicheng Army Flying School—>[[category:qq close]] [[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:白城子陸軍飛行学校]]

'''The Baicheng Army Flying School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校) was one of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in {{ill|1939|ja|昭和14年}} (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently {{ill|Tokorozawa|ja|所沢市}} Namiki), {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. Later, it moved to Heitai near {{ill|Baicheng City|ja|白城市}}, in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国}}).
In June {{ill|1944|ja|昭和19年}}, the school was relocated to {{ill|Haga District, Tochigi|ja|芳賀郡}}, currently part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}} ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in {{ill|1933|ja|昭和8年}} at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from {{ill|1935|ja|昭和10年}} at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}, with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14年「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14年(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In [[Category:January]][[Category:Search created pages]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--The Baicheng Army Flying School-->[[category:qq close]] [[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]]1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the {{ill|Aviation Inspectorate General|ja|陸軍航空総監部}}<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * {{ill|Ikuhiko Hata|ja|秦郁彦}}, ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years).
== See Also == * {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}

revision.text:
'''The Baicheng Army Flying School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校) was one of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in {{ill|1939|ja|昭和14年}} (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently {{ill|Tokorozawa|ja|所沢市}} Namiki), {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. Later, it moved to Heitai near {{ill|Baicheng City|ja|白城市}}, in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国}}).
In June {{ill|1944|ja|昭和19年}}, the school was relocated to {{ill|Haga District, Tochigi|ja|芳賀郡}}, currently part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}} ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in {{ill|1933|ja|昭和8年}} at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from {{ill|1935|ja|昭和10年}} at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}, with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14年「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14年(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In [[Category:January]][[Category:Search created pages]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] <!--The Baicheng Army Flying School-->[[category:qq close]] [[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]]1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the {{ill|Aviation Inspectorate General|ja|陸軍航空総監部}}<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * {{ill|Ikuhiko Hata|ja|秦郁彦}}, ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years).
== See Also == * {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}
{{Military of Japan}}

[[Category:Imperial Japanese Army educational institutions]] [[Category:Manchukuo military facilities]] [[Category:Defunct aviation schools in Japan]] [[Category:Baicheng history]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]] <!--The Baicheng Army Flying School-->[[category:qq close]] [[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[ja:白城子陸軍飛行学校]]

'''The Baicheng Army Flying School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校) was one of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in {{ill|1939|ja|昭和14年}} (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently {{ill|Tokorozawa|ja|所沢市}} Namiki), {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. Later, it moved to Heitai near {{ill|Baicheng City|ja|白城市}}, in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国}}).
In June {{ill|1944|ja|昭和19年}}, the school was relocated to {{ill|Haga District, Tochigi|ja|芳賀郡}}, currently part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}} ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in {{ill|1933|ja|昭和8年}} at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from {{ill|1935|ja|昭和10年}} at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}, with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14年「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14年(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In [[Category:January]][[Category:Search created pages]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]] <!--The Baicheng Army Flying School-->[[category:qq close]] [[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]]1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the {{ill|Aviation Inspectorate General|ja|陸軍航空総監部}}<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * {{ill|Ikuhiko Hata|ja|秦郁彦}}, ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years).
== See Also == * {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}

[[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]] <!--The Baicheng Army Flying School-->[[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:goodbye $1]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:goodbye $1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[ja:白城子陸軍飛行学校]]

'''The Baicheng Army Flying School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校) was one of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in {{ill|1939|ja|昭和14年}} (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently {{ill|Tokorozawa|ja|所沢市}} Namiki), {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. Later, it moved to Heitai near {{ill|Baicheng City|ja|白城市}}, in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国}}).
In June {{ill|1944|ja|昭和19年}}, the school was relocated to {{ill|Haga District, Tochigi|ja|芳賀郡}}, currently part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}} ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in {{ill|1933|ja|昭和8年}} at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from {{ill|1935|ja|昭和10年}} at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}, with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14年「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14年(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In [[Category:January]][[Category:Search created pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]] <!--The Baicheng Army Flying School-->[[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]]1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the {{ill|Aviation Inspectorate General|ja|陸軍航空総監部}}<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * {{ill|Ikuhiko Hata|ja|秦郁彦}}, ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years).
== See Also == * {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}

'''The Baicheng Army Flying School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校) was one of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in {{ill|1939|ja|昭和14年}} (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently {{ill|Tokorozawa|ja|所沢市}} Namiki), {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. Later, it moved to Heitai near {{ill|Baicheng City|ja|白城市}}, in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国}}).
In June {{ill|1944|ja|昭和19年}}, the school was relocated to {{ill|Haga District, Tochigi|ja|芳賀郡}}, currently part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}} ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in {{ill|1933|ja|昭和8年}} at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from {{ill|1935|ja|昭和10年}} at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}, with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14年「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14年(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In [[Category:January]][[Category:Search created pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] <!--The Baicheng Army Flying School-->[[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]]1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the {{ill|Aviation Inspectorate General|ja|陸軍航空総監部}}<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * {{ill|Ikuhiko Hata|ja|秦郁彦}}, ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years).
== See Also == * {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}

'''The Baicheng Army Flying School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校) was one of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in {{ill|1939|ja|昭和14年}} (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently {{ill|Tokorozawa|ja|所沢市}} Namiki), {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. Later, it moved to Heitai near {{ill|Baicheng City|ja|白城市}}, in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国}}).
In June {{ill|1944|ja|昭和19年}}, the school was relocated to {{ill|Haga District, Tochigi|ja|芳賀郡}}, currently part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}} ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in {{ill|1933|ja|昭和8年}} at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from {{ill|1935|ja|昭和10年}} at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}, with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14年「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14年(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In [[Category:January]][[Category:Search created pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] <!--The Baicheng Army Flying School-->[[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]]1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the {{ill|Aviation Inspectorate General|ja|陸軍航空総監部}}<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * {{ill|Ikuhiko Hata|ja|秦郁彦}}, ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years).
== See Also == * {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}
{{Military of Japan}}

[[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] <!--The Baicheng Army Flying School-->[[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] [[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[ja:白城子陸軍飛行学校]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also! ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''The Baicheng Army Flying School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校) was one of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in {{ill|1939|ja|昭和14年}} (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently {{ill|Tokorozawa|ja|所沢市}} Namiki), {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. Later, it moved to Heitai near {{ill|Baicheng City|ja|白城市}}, in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国}}).
In June {{ill|1944|ja|昭和19年}}, the school was relocated to {{ill|Haga District, Tochigi|ja|芳賀郡}}, currently part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}} ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in {{ill|1933|ja|昭和8年}} at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from {{ill|1935|ja|昭和10年}} at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12年}}, with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14年「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14年(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In [[Category:January]][[Category:Search created pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] <!--The Baicheng Army Flying School-->[[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]]1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the {{ill|Aviation Inspectorate General|ja|陸軍航空総監部}}<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * {{ill|Ikuhiko Hata|ja|秦郁彦}}, ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years). == See Also == * {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}
{{Military of Japan}}

[[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] <!--The Baicheng Army Flying School-->[[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] [[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[ja:白城子陸軍飛行学校]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

[[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] <!--The Baicheng Army Flying School-->[[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] [[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[ja:白城子陸軍飛行学校]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

'''The Baicheng Army Flying School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校) was one of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in {{ill|1939|ja|昭和14th year }} (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently {{ill|Tokorozawa|ja|所沢市}} Namiki), {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. Later, it moved to Heitai near {{ill|Baicheng City|ja|白城市}}, in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国}}).
In June {{ill|1944|ja|昭和19th year }}, the school was relocated to {{ill|Haga District, Tochigi|ja|芳賀郡}}, currently part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}} ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in {{ill|1933|ja|昭和8th year }} at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from {{ill|1935|ja|昭和10年}} at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12nd year }}, with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14th year 「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14th year (防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In [[Category:January]][[Category:Search created pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:11]] <!--The Baicheng Army Flying School-->[[Category:The Baicheng Army Flying School]]1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the {{ill|Aviation Inspectorate General|ja|陸軍航空総監部}}<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * {{ill|Ikuhiko Hata|ja|秦郁彦}}, ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years). == See Also == * {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}
{{Military of Japan}}

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''The Baicheng Army Flying School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校) was one of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in {{ill|1939|ja|昭和14th year }} (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently {{ill|Tokorozawa|ja|所沢市}} Namiki), {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. Later, it moved to Heitai near {{ill|Baicheng City|ja|白城市}}, in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国}}).
In June {{ill|1944|ja|昭和19th year }}, the school was relocated to {{ill|Haga District, Tochigi|ja|芳賀郡}}, currently part of {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20th year }}, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}} ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in {{ill|1933|ja|昭和8th year }} at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from {{ill|1935|ja|昭和10th year }} at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, {{ill|1937|ja|昭和12nd year }}, with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14th year 「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14th year (防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In
<!--The Baicheng Army Flying School-->1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the {{ill|Aviation Inspectorate General|ja|陸軍航空総監部}}<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * {{ill|Ikuhiko Hata|ja|秦郁彦}}, ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years). == See Also == * {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|ja|大日本帝国陸軍}}
{{Military of Japan}}
<!--The Baicheng Army Flying School-->
