revision.text:
'''The Baicheng Army Flying School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校) was one of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military academies|qq=}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently [[:en:Tokorozawa, Saitama|Tokorozawa]] Namiki), [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. Later, it moved to Heitai near [[:en:Baicheng|Baicheng City]], in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchukuo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 満洲国}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China (region)|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}).
In June [[:en:1944|1944]], the school was relocated to [[:en:Haga District, Tochigi|Haga District, Tochigi]], currently part of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July [[:en:1945|1945]], the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|qq=}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in [[:en:1933|1933]] at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|qq=}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from [[:en:1935|1935]] at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|qq=}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, [[:en:1937|1937]], with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya Army Aviation School|qq=}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|qq=}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14th year 「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Corps|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 航空兵}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military Police Corps|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 憲兵}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14th year (防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In 1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the [[:en:Inspectorate General of Aviation|Aviation Inspectorate General]]<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * [[:en:Ikuhiko Hata|Ikuhiko Hata]], ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years). == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]
{{Military of Japan}}

{{draft categories| [[Category:January]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]]
}}

In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * [[:en:Ikuhiko Hata|Ikuhiko Hata]], ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years). == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]

[[sn:The Baicheng Army Flying School]] [[ja:白城子陸軍飛行学校]]
<!--The Baicheng Army Flying School--><!--The Baicheng Army Flying School--> [[Category:Aviation schools in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese Army Educational Institutions]] [[Category:Educational organizations of Manchukuo]] [[Category:Military of Manchukuo]] [[Category:Defunct Overseas Japanese Military Facilities]] [[Category:History of Shiraoka]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:白城子陸軍飛行學校]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:The Baicheng Army Flying School]][[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11008142]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''The Baicheng Army Flying School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校) was one of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military academies|qq=|1=military school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1935049}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently [[:en:Tokorozawa, Saitama|Tokorozawa]] Namiki), [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. Later, it moved to Heitai near [[:en:Baicheng|Baicheng City]], in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchukuo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 満洲国|1=Manchukuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30623}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}).
In June [[:en:1944|1944]], the school was relocated to [[:en:Haga District, Tochigi|Haga District, Tochigi]], currently part of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July [[:en:1945|1945]], the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|qq=|1=Greater East Asia War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3846875}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in [[:en:1933|1933]] at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|qq=|1=Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360908}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from [[:en:1935|1935]] at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|qq=|1=Tokorozawa Air Field/Flight School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496798}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, [[:en:1937|1937]], with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya Army Aviation School|qq=|1=Kumagaya Army Flight School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568852}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|qq=|1=The Baicheng Army Flying School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11008142}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14th year 「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Corps|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 航空兵|1=Aviation Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613851}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military Police Corps|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 憲兵|1=military police|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210815}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14th year (防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In 1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the [[:en:Inspectorate General of Aviation|Aviation Inspectorate General]]<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * [[:en:Ikuhiko Hata|Ikuhiko Hata]], ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years). == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11008142]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''The Baicheng Army Flying School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校) was one of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military academies|qq=|1=military school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1935049}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently [[:en:Tokorozawa, Saitama|Tokorozawa]] Namiki), [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. Later, it moved to Heitai near [[:en:Baicheng|Baicheng City]], in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchukuo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 満洲国|1=Manchukuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30623}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}).
In June [[:en:1944|1944]], the school was relocated to [[:en:Haga District, Tochigi|Haga District, Tochigi]], currently part of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July [[:en:1945|1945]], the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|qq=|1=Greater East Asia War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3846875}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in [[:en:1933|1933]] at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|qq=|1=Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360908}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from [[:en:1935|1935]] at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|qq=|1=Tokorozawa Air Field/Flight School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496798}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, [[:en:1937|1937]], with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya Army Aviation School|qq=|1=Kumagaya Army Flight School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568852}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|qq=|1=The Baicheng Army Flying School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11008142}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14th year 「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Corps|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 航空兵|1=Aviation Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613851}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military Police Corps|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 憲兵|1=military police|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210815}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14th year (防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In 1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the [[:en:Inspectorate General of Aviation|Aviation Inspectorate General]]<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * [[:en:Ikuhiko Hata|Ikuhiko Hata]], ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years). == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]

Both were disbanded following the end of the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|qq=|1=Greater East Asia War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3846875}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in [[:en:1933|1933]] at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|qq=|1=Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360908}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from [[:en:1935|1935]] at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|qq=|1=Tokorozawa Air Field/Flight School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496798}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, [[:en:1937|1937]], with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya Army Aviation School|qq=|1=Kumagaya Army Flight School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568852}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|qq=|1=The Baicheng Army Flying School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11008142}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14th year 「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Corps|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 航空兵|1=Aviation Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613851}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military Police Corps|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 憲兵|1=military police|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210815}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14th year (防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In 1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the [[:en:Inspectorate General of Aviation|Aviation Inspectorate General]]<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * [[:en:Ikuhiko Hata|Ikuhiko Hata]], ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years). == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白城子陸軍飛行学校]][[zh:白城子陸軍飛行學校]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11008142]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''The Baicheng Army Flying School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校) was one of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military academies|qq=|1=military school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1935049}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently [[:en:Tokorozawa, Saitama|Tokorozawa]] Namiki), [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. Later, it moved to Heitai near [[:en:Baicheng|Baicheng City]], in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchukuo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 満洲国|1=Manchukuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30623}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}).
In June [[:en:1944|1944]], the school was relocated to [[:en:Haga District, Tochigi|Haga District, Tochigi]], currently part of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July [[:en:1945|1945]], the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|qq=|1=Greater East Asia War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3846875}}) in August of the same year. The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|1=The Baicheng Army Flying School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11008142}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Corps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 航空兵|1=Aviation Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613851}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military Police Corps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 憲兵|1=military police|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210815}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14th year (防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In 1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the [[:en:Inspectorate General of Aviation|Aviation Inspectorate General]]<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * [[:en:Ikuhiko Hata|Ikuhiko Hata]], ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years). == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]

<!--The Baicheng Army Flying School--><!--The Baicheng Army Flying School--> [[Category:Aviation schools in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese Army Educational Institutions]] [[Category:Educational organizations of Manchukuo]] [[Category:Military of Manchukuo]] [[Category:Defunct Overseas Japanese Military Facilities]] [[Category:History of Shiraoka]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:白城子陸軍飛行學校]] {{wikidata link|Q11008142}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白城子陸軍飛行学校]][[zh:白城子陸軍飛行學校]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11008142]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''The Baicheng Army Flying School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校) was one of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military academies|1=military school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1935049}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently [[:en:Tokorozawa, Saitama|Tokorozawa]] Namiki), [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. Later, it moved to Heitai near [[:en:Baicheng|Baicheng City]], in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchukuo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 満洲国|1=Manchukuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30623}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}).
In June [[:en:1944|1944]], the school was relocated to [[:en:Haga District, Tochigi|Haga District, Tochigi]], currently part of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July [[:en:1945|1945]], the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|1=Greater East Asia War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3846875}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in [[:en:1933|1933]] at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|1=Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360908}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from [[:en:1935|1935]] at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|1=Tokorozawa Air Field/Flight School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496798}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, [[:en:1937|1937]], with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya Army Aviation School|1=Kumagaya Army Flight School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568852}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|1=The Baicheng Army Flying School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11008142}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14th year 「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Corps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 航空兵|1=Aviation Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613851}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military Police Corps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 憲兵|1=military police|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210815}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14th year (防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In 1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the [[:en:Inspectorate General of Aviation|Aviation Inspectorate General]]<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * [[:en:Ikuhiko Hata|Ikuhiko Hata]], ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years). == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]

<!--The Baicheng Army Flying School--><!--The Baicheng Army Flying School--> [[Category:Aviation schools in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese Army Educational Institutions]] [[Category:Educational organizations of Manchukuo]] [[Category:Military of Manchukuo]] [[Category:Defunct Overseas Japanese Military Facilities]] [[Category:History of Shiraoka]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:白城子陸軍飛行學校]] {{wikidata link|Q11008142}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata has English label - based on Wikidata English label
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白城子陸軍飛行学校]][[zh:白城子陸軍飛行學校]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11008142]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''The Baicheng Army Flying School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校) was one of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military academies|1=military school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1935049}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently [[:en:Tokorozawa, Saitama|Tokorozawa]] Namiki), [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. Later, it moved to Heitai near [[:en:Baicheng|Baicheng City]], in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchukuo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 満洲国|1=Manchukuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30623}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}).
In June [[:en:1944|1944]], the school was relocated to [[:en:Haga District, Tochigi|Haga District, Tochigi]], currently part of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July [[:en:1945|1945]], the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|1=Greater East Asia War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3846875}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in [[:en:1933|1933]] at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|1=Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360908}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from [[:en:1935|1935]] at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|1=Tokorozawa Air Field/Flight School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496798}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, [[:en:1937|1937]], with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya Army Aviation School|1=Kumagaya Army Flight School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568852}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|1=The Baicheng Army Flying School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11008142}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14th year 「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Corps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 航空兵|1=Aviation Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613851}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military Police Corps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 憲兵|1=military police|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210815}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14th year (防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In 1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the [[:en:Inspectorate General of Aviation|Aviation Inspectorate General]]<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * [[:en:Ikuhiko Hata|Ikuhiko Hata]], ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years). == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]
{{Military of Japan}}

Both were disbanded following the end of the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|1=Greater East Asia War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3846875}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in [[:en:1933|1933]] at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|1=Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360908}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from [[:en:1935|1935]] at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|1=Tokorozawa Air Field/Flight School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496798}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, [[:en:1937|1937]], with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya Army Aviation School|1=Kumagaya Army Flight School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568852}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|1=The Baicheng Army Flying School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11008142}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14th year 「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Corps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 航空兵|1=Aviation Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613851}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military Police Corps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 憲兵|1=military police|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210815}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14th year (防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In 1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the [[:en:Inspectorate General of Aviation|Aviation Inspectorate General]]<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * [[:en:Ikuhiko Hata|Ikuhiko Hata]], ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years). == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白城子陸軍飛行学校]][[zh:白城子陸軍飛行學校]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11008142]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''The Baicheng Army Flying School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校) was one of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military academies|1=military school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1935049}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently [[:en:Tokorozawa, Saitama|Tokorozawa]] Namiki), [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. Later, it moved to Heitai near [[:en:Baicheng|Baicheng City]], in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchukuo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 満洲国|1=Manchukuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30623}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}).
In June [[:en:1944|1944]], the school was relocated to [[:en:Haga District, Tochigi|Haga District, Tochigi]], currently part of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July [[:en:1945|1945]], the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|1=Greater East Asia War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3846875}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.

The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|1=The Baicheng Army Flying School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11008142}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14th year 「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Corps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 航空兵|1=Aviation Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613851}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military Police Corps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 憲兵|1=military police|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210815}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14th year (防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In 1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the [[:en:Inspectorate General of Aviation|Aviation Inspectorate General]]<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * [[:en:Ikuhiko Hata|Ikuhiko Hata]], ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years). == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白城子陸軍飛行学校]][[zh:白城子陸軍飛行學校]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11008142]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''The Baicheng Army Flying School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校) was one of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military academies|1=military school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1935049}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently [[:en:Tokorozawa, Saitama|Tokorozawa]] Namiki), [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. Later, it moved to Heitai near [[:en:Baicheng|Baicheng City]], in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchukuo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 満洲国|1=Manchukuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30623}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}).
In June [[:en:1944|1944]], the school was relocated to [[:en:Haga District, Tochigi|Haga District, Tochigi]], currently part of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July [[:en:1945|1945]], the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|1=Greater East Asia War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3846875}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in [[:en:1933|1933]] at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|1=Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360908}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from [[:en:1935|1935]] at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|1=Tokorozawa Air Field/Flight School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496798}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, [[:en:1937|1937]], with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya Army Aviation School|1=Kumagaya Army Flight School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568852}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|1=The Baicheng Army Flying School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11008142}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14th year 「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Corps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 航空兵|1=Aviation Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613851}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military Police Corps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 憲兵|1=military police|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210815}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14th year (防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In 1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the [[:en:Inspectorate General of Aviation|Aviation Inspectorate General]]<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * [[:en:Ikuhiko Hata|Ikuhiko Hata]], ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years). == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]

By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14th year (防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In 1940, the relocation was completed.

The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * [[:en:Ikuhiko Hata|Ikuhiko Hata]], ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years). == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白城子陸軍飛行学校]][[zh:白城子陸軍飛行學校]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11008142]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''The Baicheng Army Flying School''' (白城子陸軍飛行学校) was one of the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]'s {{ill|military academies|ja|軍学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/military academies|1=military school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1935049}}, primarily engaged in aviation navigation education and research. Established in [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently [[:en:Tokorozawa, Saitama|Tokorozawa]] Namiki), [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. Later, it moved to Heitai near [[:en:Baicheng|Baicheng City]], in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchukuo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 満洲国|1=Manchukuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30623}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}).
In June [[:en:1944|1944]], the school was relocated to [[:en:Haga District, Tochigi|Haga District, Tochigi]], currently part of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July [[:en:1945|1945]], the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|1=Greater East Asia War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3846875}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in [[:en:1933|1933]] at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|1=Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360908}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from [[:en:1935|1935]] at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|1=Tokorozawa Air Field/Flight School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496798}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, [[:en:1937|1937]], with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya Army Aviation School|1=Kumagaya Army Flight School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568852}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|1=The Baicheng Army Flying School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11008142}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14th year 「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Corps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 航空兵|1=Aviation Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613851}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military Police Corps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 憲兵|1=military police|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210815}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14th year (防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In 1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the [[:en:Inspectorate General of Aviation|Aviation Inspectorate General]]<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * [[:en:Ikuhiko Hata|Ikuhiko Hata]], ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years). == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]

Established in [[:en:1939|1939]] (Shōwa 14) in July, the school's headquarters were initially located in Tokorozawa Town (currently [[:en:Tokorozawa, Saitama|Tokorozawa]] Namiki), [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. Later, it moved to Heitai near [[:en:Baicheng|Baicheng City]], in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchukuo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 満洲国|1=Manchukuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30623}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}).
In June [[:en:1944|1944]], the school was relocated to [[:en:Haga District, Tochigi|Haga District, Tochigi]], currently part of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July [[:en:1945|1945]], the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|1=Greater East Asia War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3846875}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in [[:en:1933|1933]] at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|1=Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360908}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from [[:en:1935|1935]] at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|1=Tokorozawa Air Field/Flight School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496798}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, [[:en:1937|1937]], with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya Army Aviation School|1=Kumagaya Army Flight School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568852}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|1=The Baicheng Army Flying School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11008142}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14th year 「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Corps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 航空兵|1=Aviation Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613851}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military Police Corps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 憲兵|1=military police|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210815}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14th year (防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In 1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the [[:en:Inspectorate General of Aviation|Aviation Inspectorate General]]<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * [[:en:Ikuhiko Hata|Ikuhiko Hata]], ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years). == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]

Later, it moved to Heitai near [[:en:Baicheng|Baicheng City]], in {{ill|Manchukuo|ja|満州国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchukuo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 満洲国|1=Manchukuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30623}} (now part of northeastern {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}}).
In June [[:en:1944|1944]], the school was relocated to [[:en:Haga District, Tochigi|Haga District, Tochigi]], currently part of [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], and reorganized into the '''Utsunomiya Army Training Aviation Division'''. In July [[:en:1945|1945]], the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was restructured into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both were disbanded following the end of the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] ({{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|1=Greater East Asia War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3846875}}) in August of the same year. This article also describes the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division and related entities.
== History == === Hakujōshi Army Aviation School === Education and research on army aviation navigation began in [[:en:1933|1933]] at the {{ill|Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|ja|下志津陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|1=Shimoshizu Army Aviation School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360908}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2958372/3}} Official Gazette No. 1900, May 5, 1933]</ref>, and from [[:en:1935|1935]] at the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|ja|所沢陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokorozawa Army Aviation School|1=Tokorozawa Air Field/Flight School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496798}}<ref>Army Directive No. 10, [{{NDLDC|2959051/3}} Official Gazette No. 2572, July 30, 1935]</ref>. On October 1, [[:en:1937|1937]], with the closure of the Tokorozawa Army Aviation School, navigation education and research were temporarily transferred to the {{ill|Kumagaya Army Aviation School|ja|熊谷陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya Army Aviation School|1=Kumagaya Army Flight School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568852}}<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|A03022139200|御署名原本・昭和十二年・勅令第六〇〇号・熊谷陸軍飛行学校令中改正(国立公文書館)}}</ref>, continuing as a branch facility in Tokorozawa.
The {{ill|Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|ja|白城子陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakujōshi Army Aviation School|1=The Baicheng Army Flying School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11008142}} was established on July 1, 1939, under Army Directive (Land Regulation No. 20). Its mission was defined as educating students in navigation sciences and techniques essential for navigation duties while also conducting research, testing, and evaluation of necessary weaponry and equipment<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01004563900|昭和14th year 「密大日記」第1冊(防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>.
The school admitted students under the following categories (as of July 1939): * '''Navigation Students:''' Officers from the {{ill|Aviation Corps|ja|航空兵科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Corps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 航空兵|1=Aviation Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613851}} who had trained as pilots for approximately two years. * '''Special Students:''' Communication-trained aviation officers and non-commissioned officers. * '''Others:''' Temporary recruits from all corps, excluding the {{ill|Military Police Corps|ja|憲兵科|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military Police Corps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 憲兵|1=military police|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q210815}}, for special training.
Initially, the school remained in Tokorozawa while preparations were underway<ref name=source1>『所沢陸軍飛行場史』46頁</ref>. By October 1939, the facility was relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo<ref>{{アジア歴史資料センター|C01001711600|大日記甲輯昭和14th year (防衛省防衛研究所)}}</ref>. In 1940, the relocation was completed.
=== Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division === In June 1944, worsening war conditions prompted the military to militarize five flight schools and one maintenance school under the supervision of the [[:en:Inspectorate General of Aviation|Aviation Inspectorate General]]<ref>『陸軍軍戦備』428頁</ref>. The Hakujōshi Army Aviation School was closed and reorganized into the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division<ref name=Kessen317>『本土防空決戦』317頁</ref>. The division was tasked with training personnel and simultaneously preparing for defensive operations in Japan.
In 1945, as part of restructuring efforts, the Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division was reorganized into the '''Training Aviation Division Headquarters''' and the '''6th Training Aviation Regiment'''. Both entities were demobilized after the war ended in August 1945.
== Timeline == * 1939: Hakujōshi Army Aviation School established in Tokorozawa. * 1940: Relocated to Heitai near Hakujōshi in Manchukuo. * 1944: Relocated to Haga District, Tochigi, and reorganized as Utsunomiya Training Aviation Division. * 1945: Division further reorganized into the Training Aviation Division Headquarters and the 6th Training Aviation Regiment.
== References == * [[:en:Ikuhiko Hata|Ikuhiko Hata]], ''Japan's Army and Navy Encyclopedia'' (2nd edition), University of Tokyo Press, 2005. * Imperial Japanese Army Organizational Records (various years). == See Also == * [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]]
